What it is for: Single web entry point. Every HTTP request lands here; it boots Kirby (require kirby/bootstrap.php), which then dispatches to a template / route / panel. Standard Kirby convention — the file Deco could not exist without but does not own.
PHP dependency manifest. Pins the Kirby version and declares the PHP autoloader. Exists because Kirby is installed via Composer.

Tells git which paths not to track: Kirby's runtime artefacts (media/, site/cache, site/sessions, site/accounts beyond stubs), Deco's local snapshot store (library/* except .gitkeep), and per-page content JSON (content/*/instances.json, groups.json, page.json — Deco data is local-only by design). Also OS junk.
Deco's semver (e.g. 0.8.288). Read by site/config/config.php and used as ?v= cache-buster on every CSS/JS include so a bump invalidates browsers automatically. Bumped on every iteration of edits.

On-disk snapshot-envelope schema version (currently 1). Bumped only when the library/<name>/meta.json envelope changes shape incompatibly. Snapshots → Load refuses on mismatch. NOT the same as the per-page CONTENT_SCHEMA_VERSION (currently 11) defined inside scripts/migrate-content.php.
Multi-thousand-line briefing for any Claude / human picking up Deco development. Architecture, decisions, versions, diagnostic tools, gotchas. Read on session start.
Project-level Claude instructions: read HANDOFF first, behavioural rules, schema-version bump protocol, progressive-disclosure UI rule.
Free-form working notes. Not consumed by any code.
Older filesystem inventory (pre-Deco-naming, pre-Kirby/Deco split). Superseded by this file but kept for reference.
Earlier draft of the same. Same role as project-hierarchy.csv.
Apple Numbers spreadsheet variant of the same inventory. Manual reference only.
Standalone HTML page (no Kirby in the loop) used to prototype browser-zoom counter-scaling for the editor canvas. Diagnostic / R&D artefact.
Variant of zoom.html with additional test scaffolding. Same role.
The entire vendored Kirby CMS (src, panel, dependencies, i18n, etc.). Do not edit. Updated only by replacing this directory with a new Kirby release.
Where Kirby writes generated image thumbnails and other on-demand cached media. Holds only an index.html directory-listing stub in this project (Kirby's own files are gitignored).
Maintenance scripts owned by Deco.

Per-page content-shape migration runner. Defines CONTENT_SCHEMA_VERSION (currently 11) and the chain of v(N) → v(N+1) callables that upgrade each content/<page>/page.json on load. The other half of the two-tier schema-versioning model (the snapshot envelope is the other axis, see SCHEMA_VERSION).

Local snapshot store. Each subdirectory is one named snapshot of Deco's content. Created by the editor's Snapshots → Save. Gitignored except for .gitkeep. Current snapshots present: "Move text", "Test following objects", "W textblock".
All Deco-owned client assets: CSS, JS, fonts, images. Served as-is by the webserver.
Public-site stylesheet. Loaded on every Deco-rendered page. Defines design tokens (--accent, --text, font families) referenced everywhere.
Editor-only stylesheet. Loaded by site/templates/draw.php; never reaches public pages. Styles the toolbar, sidebar, panels, block rows, follow-chain badges, etc.

Runtime renderer. Reads the inlined <script id="lines-data"> payload that lines-layer.php writes, picks a viewport class, paints SVG paths, drives GSAP/ScrollTrigger animations, runs the multi-hop follow-chain resolver, etc. Loaded on every public page.
The editor itself. Drawing tools, selection, group/master/palette UIs, behavior block panels, follow-this-object UI, snapshots, orphans dialog, save endpoint client. Loaded only on /dev/draw.
Source for the editor's font-bundle bookmarklet (Slice 2a-2, v0.8.200). Wrapped at runtime by /dev/draw/fonts-bundle to POST locally-resolved font URLs back to the site.
GreenSock Animation Platform. Drives all of Deco's timed/scroll animation. Vendored, not Kirby.
GSAP plugin: ties tweens to scroll position. Vendored.
GSAP plugin: drag-and-drop primitives. Vendored; currently unused but kept for future.
GSAP plugin: physics-y inertial dragging. Vendored; currently unused.
Local font files (TTF/OTF). manifest.json describes what's available. Read by the editor's font-bundle / runtime font-loading.
Lists local fonts with their files + display names.
Static images bundled with the design. Currently two test PNGs; SVG-import / image-drop flows would deposit files here.

Kirby convention root for everything that defines THIS site (templates, snippets, plugins, blueprints, config, runtime dirs). All contents below are Deco-owned even though the directory's existence is a Kirby convention.

Kirby's boot config — and Deco's HTTP routes: POST /dev/draw/save, /dev/draw/library/{list,save,load}, /dev/draw/font-bundle, /dev/draw/fonts-bundle. Exposes option('version') and option('schemaVersion') so PHP/JS code can read VERSION + SCHEMA_VERSION. Authoritative wiring point between the two systems.
Kirby Panel site-level schema (title etc.). Used only if the Kirby Panel is enabled — Deco does not use the Panel today, so this is essentially inert.
Default page schema for the Kirby Panel. Same: inert because Deco does not use the Panel.
Deco's PHP plugin. Registered with Kirby as 'site/deco'. This IS Deco's server-side core.

Deco's server-side helpers: deco_load_palette, deco_load_class_data, deco_resolve_instance (the instance → master compose step including pass-through of followsMasterId), deco_viewbox_attr, deco_default_dims, deco_load_classes, deco_load_page_config, deco_load_masters, etc. Bridges on-disk content into the runtime payload format. Also where save / library / font-bundle route handlers do their work.
Kirby convention directory for reusable view fragments. All snippets here are Deco-defined.
<head> snippet: title, viewport meta, style.css include (cache-busted with ?v=VERSION). Deco content, Kirby convention.
Closing tags + app.js include (cache-busted). Deco content, Kirby convention.

Builds the runtime data payload (classes + palette + per-class instances/groups + page config + version) and emits the <svg id="lines-layer"> + <script id="lines-data"> blocks that app.js reads. The bridge that turns content/ on-disk JSON into something app.js can consume.
Page-content snippet for one of three button shapes used in design pages.
Page-content snippet for one of three button shapes.
Page-content snippet for one of three button shapes.
Empty directory-listing stub. Kirby/webserver convention: prevents the webserver from auto-indexing a sensitive directory.
Kirby convention directory for page templates. All templates here are Deco-defined.
Fallback page template — wraps header + content + footer. Used when a page doesn't specify a more specific template.
Home page template — adds the lines-layer SVG so the runtime can render on /. Deco's main page wrapper.

The editor's template. Renders the editor toolbar + sidebar + canvas, inlines a much richer payload than the runtime (pages, classes, masters, palette, trigger suggestions, image sources, version), loads dev-draw.js. Reached at /dev/draw. The Deco editor lives here.
Where Kirby would store Panel user credentials when the Panel is in use. Contains only index.html stub; no user accounts in this project. Git ignores anything else here.
Kirby's HTTP/page cache (when enabled). Only the stub here. Gitignored otherwise.
Where Kirby keeps session files for logged-in users. Only the stub here. Gitignored otherwise.

Kirby convention directory for site content (Kirby reads here to know what pages exist). Below this point, the .txt files are Kirby-shaped (page identity) and the .json files are Deco-shaped (Deco's actual data).
Kirby site-level content (title, etc.) in Kirby's text format. Kirby reads on boot.
Top-level Deco config: useClasses + per-class dims (pageW/H, canvasW/H) for the canvas. Carries _schemaVersion (currently 11). Used as the default config when a sub-page does not define its own.
Site-wide Deco data (not per-page).
Class definitions: id + display name for narrow / medium / wide viewport classes.

The master library. Canonical visual definitions: each entry is one logical object (id, name, kind, params/points/segments/d, stroke, width, scope). Every instance in every class on every page references a master here.
Site-wide colour palette (v4+ canonical location). Editor reads and writes here.
Resolved font-bundle entries (URLs / files) for the editor's font system. Written by the /dev/draw/font-bundle endpoint.
Kirby page-metadata file that makes /dev/draw exist as a Kirby route — that is how the Deco editor's draw.php template gets reached. Deco's editor depends on it; Kirby is what actually parses it.
Kirby's 'error' page (404 fallback). error.txt is Kirby; the per-class instances.json files are Deco (currently empty — error page has no Deco content).
Kirby page-metadata for the error page.
Per-class Deco instance records for the error page. Currently empty lists.
Per-page Deco config for the error page (uses defaults from content/page.json today).
The home page directory.
Kirby page-metadata for / (home page title, etc.).
Per-page Deco config for the home page: useClasses + dims.
Per-class instance records for the home page. Each entry: { id, masterId, groupId, positionOffset, behaviors[], overrides, followsMasterId? }. The 'skin' referencing a master. One file per class.
Per-class group records for the home page. Each group: id, name, hidden, defaults (fallback translateX/Y, rotate, drawIn applied to lines in this group).
Top-level per-class instance files. Currently empty lists. Legacy from an earlier site-level shape; harmless but inactive. Safe to delete after a verification pass.
